LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT THE HERTZ CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO FEBRUARY 24, 2014 TO GIVE OPPOSING PARTY THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. OPPOSING PARTY MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE OPPOSITION PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JANUARY 22, 2014 AT 4:00 PM OR THE MOTION WILL BE GRANTED. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT THE HERTZ CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE COMPLAINT AND JUDGMENT IN THE SMALL CLAIMS ACTION (CSM-12-840669) PURSUANT TO EVIDENCE CODE SEC. 452(D). BOTH THE SMALL CLAIMS COMPLAINT AND THE COMPLAINT IN THIS ACTION ASSERT CLAIMS FOR NEGLIGENCE, INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS, PERSONAL INJURY, AND PROPERTY DAMAGES ARISING OUT OF THE SAME CAR ACCIDENT. RES JUDICATA BARS PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS IN THIS ACTION. (SEE SANDERSON V. NIEMANN (1941) 17 CAL.2D 563, 573, "A JUDGMENT OF ANY COURT WHETHER OF HIGH OR LOW JURISDICTION, AND OF RECORD OR NOT, CONSTITUTES A COMPLETE BAR AGAINST A SECOND SUIT ON THE SAME CAUSE OF ACTION.") A GENERAL DEMURRER LIES WHERE THE FACTS ALLEGED IN THE COMPLAINT OR MATTERS JUDICIALLY NOTICED SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF IS SEEKING RELIEF FROM THE SAME DEFENDANT ON THE SAME CAUSE OF ACTION. (BOEKEN V. PHILIP MORRIS (2010) 48 CAL.4TH 788.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976
MINI MINUTES FOR FEB-24-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Plaintiff ALAN MOSS appearing on his own behalf,
No appearance by defaulted defendant KENDRA R. GILES,

Plaintiff sworn and examined.

Damages: $20,968.30
Pre-Judgment Interest: $0
Attorney's Fees: $0
Costs: $902.75
Total Judgment in the amount of $21,871.05

ATTY. JAMES J. SLONE OF THE MCDOWELL, SHAW, COLMAN & GARCIA, TEL. NO. (925) 210-1300 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT THE HERTZ CORPORATION BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT THE HERTZ CORPORATION'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend. The Court takes judicial notice of the complaint and judgment in the small claims action (CSM-12-840669) pursuant to Evidence Code sec. 452(d). Both the small claims complaint and the complaint in this action assert claims for negligence, infliction of emotional distress, personal injury, and property damages arising out of the same car accident. Res judicata bars Plaintiff's claims in this action. (See Sanderson v. Niemann (1941) 17 Cal.2d 563, 573, "a judgment of any court whether of high or low jurisdiction, and of record or not, constitutes a complete bar against a second suit on the same cause of action.") A general demurrer lies where the facts alleged in the complaint or matters judicially noticed show that plaintiff is seeking relief from the same defendant on the same cause of action. (Boeken v. Philip Morris (2010) 48 Cal.4th 788.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA hERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg
